summary: The case reveals a cognitive-behavioral loop where anticipatory social evaluation fear (C02) drives repetitive proofreading (C01), which in turn delays responses (C03) and fosters the belief that others interpret delays as ignoring (C04). A vague internal standard for message adequacy (C05) sustains repeated edits, while time spent on messaging (C06) impairs social functioning. The proposed levers span bio/psycho/social domains and their interactions, targeting observable, modifiable predictors (not labels) to reduce checking, speed up appropriate responding, recalibrate internal standards, and lessen social-threat appraisals, with practical measurement plans and short-to-mid term horizons.

solutions.predictor_variable: Structured message drafting templates with a capped checking rule and a 'good-enough' sending threshold
solutions.solution_candidate: Provide a library of concise message templates (e.g., apologize, clarify, respond with key point, check-in) plus a simple rule: do not reread more than 2 times; set a fixed 3-minute drafting window before sending. Use an optional one-click 'send' button after the timer or templates are filled. Reinforce with a quick in-app nudge that the goal is clear, respectful communication, not perfection.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Directly reduces repetitive checking (C01) by externalizing content and constraining edits; standardizes send-ready criteria (C05) through templates and time-bound rules, lowering internal thresholds that drive over-editing.
solutions.measurement: Average edits per outgoing message; time spent drafting; proportion of messages sent after 2 edits or within the timer; self-rated adequacy before sending (0-10 scale).
solutions.mapping_query: Maps to C01 (checking) and C05 (threshold for adequacy) via externalized templates and time-bound sending rules.

solutions.predictor_variable: Cognitive reappraisal and evidence-based challenge of fears of negative evaluation
solutions.solution_candidate: A brief CBT-informed module (4–6 sessions or app-based micro-lessons) focusing on identifying automatic thoughts like 'they’ll think I’m stupid', testing evidence, generating alternative interpretations, and rehearsing responses to perceived judgement. Include homework to document one social interaction per day and rate anxiety before/during. Optional brief coaching or peer-supported practice.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Targets anticipatory anxiety (C02) which fuels checking (C01). By reframing threat and providing practical cognitive tools, both fear and checking are expected to decline.
solutions.measurement: State anxiety before sending (0-10); frequency of checking; self-reported confidence in messages (0-10).
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns beyond standard therapeutic risk monitoring (seek help if worsening anxiety or depressive symptoms).
solutions.mapping_query: Targets C02 (fear of evaluation) and C01 (checking) via cognitive restructuring and behavioral exercises.

solutions.predictor_variable: Explicit reply-time goals and daily messaging time-blocks
solutions.solution_candidate: Establish a social-communication protocol: (a) set a non-urgent reply target (e.g., respond within 60 minutes during daytime); (b) allocate two dedicated messaging blocks per day; (c) use a one-line status to set expectations when delays occur. Provide reminders and a simple app-based timer to enforce blocks.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Addresses delayed replies (C03) by creating predictable, attainable response timelines; reduces time sunk in ongoing checking and preserves social functioning (C06).
solutions.measurement: Median reply latency from message receipt to sending; number of messages sent within target window; daily time spent on messaging.
solutions.mapping_query: Directly maps to C03 (delay) and C06 (time impact) through structured timing rules.

solutions.predictor_variable: Reattribution training to normalize delays and reduce misinterpretations
solutions.solution_candidate: Teach and practice flexible attributions for others’ behavior (e.g., ‘they’re busy’ or ‘they value accuracy, like me’) and communicate norms that thoughtful replies take time. Include a short refresher script to send when worried about being perceived as rude.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces perceived social consequence (C04) and fear (C02) by reframing social interpretation; may also decrease delays (C03) by reducing threat-driven avoidance.
solutions.measurement: Belief about others’ interpretations (0-10); frequency of delayed responses; self-reported fear before replying (0-10).
solutions.mapping_query: Addresses C04 via social-attribution reframing; also supports C02/C03 via reduced threat and avoidance.

solutions.predictor_variable: Metacognitive threshold tuning for 'good enough' and satisficing in messaging
solutions.solution_candidate: A brief metacognitive training that helps individuals set explicit adequacy criteria (e.g., send when 80% of key points are covered) and practice rapid decision-making in messaging. Provide a simple checklist (key points, tone, clarity) to self-administer before sending.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Directly targets the uncertain threshold (C05) and, by providing a concrete criterion, reduces excessive checking (C01).
solutions.measurement: Internal adequacy criterion used (self-report); edits per message; time to send after drafting begins.
solutions.mapping_query: Specifically mitigates C05 (threshold for sending) and C01 (checking).

solutions.predictor_variable: Bio-psycho-social stress regulation: sleep, mindfulness, and brief pre-message regulation
solutions.solution_candidate: Integrate daily 5–10 minute mindfulness/relaxation practice and consistent sleep/wake routines; include a 1–2 minute breathing exercise before drafting messages. Emphasize stress reduction to lower cognitive load and rumination.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces cognitive arousal and rumination (C02) and improves cognitive efficiency, potentially reducing time spent on messaging and need for excessive checking (C01) and delays (C03 indirectly via improved regulation).
solutions.measurement: Evening wake time consistency; sleep duration; pre-message anxiety rating (0-10); edits per message.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns beyond standard mindfulness practice guidelines.
solutions.mapping_query: Links to C02 (anxiety), C01 (checking), and C06 (time impact) through stress regulation and sleep hygiene.
